title: Accessible Reader Surface, Untrusted Content, and Safe Links · Execution Trace: Feed Go Untrusted Content
summary: The UI injects feed HTML, opens deceptive links, depends on card color, loses focus after updates, hides source context, and forces infinite scroll. Predict the reader decision, feed or API contract, Go and PostgreSQL ownership, bounded failure, and observable evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the feed go untrusted content increment in keyboard-first content-safe feed reader. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.
Generate bounded snippets from safe text or sanitize marked fragments and expose matched terms without unsafe feed markup.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Predict Feed Go Untrusted Content · Theory Feed Go Untrusted Content
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go team handles case 6863 during a multilingual university archive import. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence; failed, canceled, stale, and cross-tenant operations must preserve the prior admitted state; then change one tenant, cursor, reader-state, authorization, cache, or accessibility condition without leaking state. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, XML libraries, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, race, load, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Treat titles, authors, summaries, HTML, images, categories, and URLs as untrusted publisher input through render-time safety boundaries.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and Go Build Feed Go Untrusted Content evidence · Theory Feed Go Untrusted Content
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: feed-go-untrusted-content-1j3p1m" that uses case 3311 to prove content-safe semantic reader structure, keyboard and focus behavior, reflow, and non-color evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, parse through native XML libraries, contact publishers, access PostgreSQL, read host files, handle real signals, launch unbounded goroutines, or claim race, load, accessibility, container, backup, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, pgx 5.10.0, goose 3.27.2, PostgreSQL 18.4, original fixtures, controlled RoundTrippers and httptest servers, disposable databases, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected images, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, and release gates.
